content.text: Beta graduation is the formal gate between a limited-availability GenAI feature and its general availability (GA) release. Without explicit graduation criteria, features drift from beta to GA by default — nobody pulls the ripcord when quality is marginal, because there is no agreed definition of what "good enough" means. A decision forum with a defined quorum and binding criteria makes graduation a deliberate act.

attrs.alt: Beta graduation criteria matrix: blocking, important, and advisory tiers across quality, reliability, and cost dimensions

content.text: The Three Tiers of Graduation Criteria
Not all criteria carry the same weight. Treating them uniformly either blocks graduation on trivial issues or allows critical gaps to be waived. The three-tier model makes the trade-off explicit:
Hard requirement — no graduation without it
Nobody — criterion must be met or feature stays in beta
Strong expectation — miss requires a mitigation plan
Plan with owner + deadline must be approved before vote
Best practice — miss is noted but does not block
Noted in decision record, monitored post-graduation

The distinction between "blocking" and "important" is the most consequential design decision. Safety and cost-cap criteria are always blocking. Quality and reliability metrics that have clear mitigation paths may be important rather than blocking, allowing graduation to proceed with an agreed remediation plan.
Safety criteria are unconditional. There is no scenario in which a feature with a confirmed safety incident in its 90-day beta window should graduate to GA.

Two cost criteria are blocking because they represent unbounded financial risk: uncapped cost vectors (where a single user action can trigger arbitrary spend) and actual cost exceeding the approved business case by more than 20%.

Manually assembling graduation criteria from dashboards and documents immediately before the forum is error-prone. A criteria evaluator script runs against live metrics and produces a structured result that can be presented at the forum.
The graduation decision forum is a structured meeting, not a consensus discussion. It has a defined quorum and a defined decision rule.
Runs the meeting, signs the decision record
Reviews adversarial eval and safety incident record

Quorum requires all four quorum-required roles to be present. If the SRE lead or Security reviewer is absent, the forum is rescheduled, not conducted by proxy — their domains are too consequential to be represented secondhand.

## Beta Graduation Forum — [Feature Name]
**Duration:** 60 minutes
**Chair:** [PM Name]

### 1. Evidence presentation (20 min)
- Engineering lead: present automated criteria evaluation output
- SRE lead: present reliability and incident summary
- Safety reviewer: confirm adversarial eval and safety gate result
- Finance: confirm cost approval (5 min)

### 2. Blocking criteria review (10 min)
- Walk through each blocking criterion
- If any blocking criterion failed: discussion ends, forum rescheduled
  with a remediation deadline

### 3. Important criteria review (10 min)
- Walk through each important criterion
- For each miss: is there a proposed mitigation plan?
  - If yes: forum chair approves or requests revision
  - If no: owner must provide plan before the vote proceeds

### 4. Advisory criteria acknowledgment (5 min)
- Note any advisory misses in the decision record
- No discussion needed unless a miss is unexpectedly large

### 5. Vote and decision (5 min)
- Three outcomes: Graduate / Extend Beta / Deprecate
- Simple majority of voting members
- Security reviewer holds veto on safety-gated criteria only
- Chair records the outcome and signs the decision record live

### 6. Post-graduation commitments (10 min)
- Assign owners for mitigation plans on important misses
- Schedule next review date
- Notify stakeholders
All blocking criteria pass; important misses have approved mitigation plans
Feature moves to GA, mitigation plans tracked to completion
One or more blocking criteria failed, but failure is addressable within 30 days
New graduation forum scheduled with specific remediation deadline
Feature works well for a subset of use cases; fails outside that subset
GA for the passing subset; failing use cases re-enter roadmap
Blocking criteria cannot be met within a reasonable timeline, or business case no longer holds

"Extend beta" is not a failure. Beta exists precisely so that problems can be found before GA. A feature that correctly stays in beta for an extra 30 days while a safety or reliability issue is resolved is the process working as intended.
The decision record is the legal and operational record of the graduation decision. It must be written before the forum ends, signed by the chair, and stored in the team's permanent documentation.

# Beta Graduation Decision Record
**Feature:** GenAI Document QA v2
**Forum date:** 2026-07-15
**Chair:** [PM Name]
**Decision:** ✅ Graduate to GA

## Attendees and votes
| Role | Name | Vote |
|------|------|------|
| PM (Chair) | Jane Smith | Graduate |
| Engineering lead | Alex Chen | Graduate |
| SRE lead | Sam Patel | Graduate |
| Security reviewer | Jordan Lee | Graduate (safety gate passed) |
| Finance | Casey Brown | Advisory — cost approved |

## Blocking criteria result
✅ Safety: 0 incidents, adversarial eval passed, PII: 0
✅ Reliability: SLO 99.7% (90d), 0 open P0 incidents, all runbooks complete
✅ Cost: no uncapped vector, actual cost 108% of forecast (within 120% threshold)

## Important criteria result
✅ Eval score: 4.2 / 5.0 (target: ≥ 4.0)
✅ On-call load: 1.4 hours/week (target: < 2h)
⚠️ P2 items open: 7 (target: < 5)
  → Mitigation: @alex-chen will close 5 of 7 by 2026-07-31;
    2 deferred to Q3 sprint as tech debt (non-user-impacting)
    Approved by forum chair.

## Advisory criteria notes
• Adoption: 73% of 90-day target (target: ≥ 80%)
  → Onboarding A/B test in progress; no action required for graduation
• NPS: +2 vs. pre-launch baseline ✅

## Post-graduation commitments
| Commitment | Owner | Due |
|------------|-------|-----|
| Close 5 open P2 items | @alex-chen | 2026-07-31 |
| Onboarding A/B test results | @pm-name | 2026-08-01 |
| Quarterly review | @pm-name | 2026-10-15 |

**Chair signature:** Jane Smith — 2026-07-15
Common failures in beta graduation processes:
Team decides what 'good enough' means at the graduation forum
Criteria are rationalized to match current state, not set to ensure quality
Forum held with SRE lead absent, notes shared via email
SRE's domain not represented; reliability risks can be approved without scrutiny
Forum delays graduation by weeks debating adoption percentages
Adoption is a business metric — missing it is a signal, not a gate
Decision record written after the fact
PM writes a summary two weeks later from memory
Decision rationale drifts; original trade-offs are lost
Feature just stops being called 'beta' with no formal forum
No accountability, no mitigation plans, no post-graduation commitments
Beta graduation criteria and a decision forum make the transition from beta to GA a deliberate, evidence-based act:
— blocking (must pass), important (miss requires a plan), advisory (miss is noted)
— no confirmed incident, no uncapped cost vector, adversarial eval passed
— run the evaluation script before the forum so the meeting makes decisions, not dashboards
— SRE lead and security reviewer must be present; their domains cannot be proxied
— Graduate / Extend beta / Deprecate. Extend beta is a success mode, not a failure
— signed in the room, stored permanently; not reconstructed from memory
— graduation criteria must be agreed at launch, not at the graduation forum
